mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks, providing a standardized approach to agent communication and management. Use Kagenti when you need a robust platform for AI agents that ensures secure and efficient operations.

steps.title: Add Kagenti Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Kagenti Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Kagenti repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Kagenti to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Kagenti.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> Installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Kagenti to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

```

> Creates an example agent resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is Ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> Lists the KAgents in the 'kagenti' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to the KAgent resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your KAgent resources. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> Backs up the current KAgent resources.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KAgent resources are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the KAgent resource is created and the correct namespace is being used.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image for the KAgent is not available in the container registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is correctly tagged and available in the registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release for Kagenti has not been installed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Kagenti using the Helm install command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KAgent pod is not in a running state due to resource constraints or configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KAgent resources listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get kagents -n kagenti'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
